title: The Greenway Affair
overview: A Belgian detective with an extraordinary gift for order and method unravels a poison murder at a Devon country house — based on the classic story Jenny read at 14.

title: Miss Marple: A Murder Is Announced
overview: A spinster sleuth from St Mary Mead investigates after a newspaper announces a murder — and a murder duly occurs in a Chipping Cleghorn cottage.

title: Enchanted April
overview: Four very different Englishwomen rent an Italian castle for a quiet April holiday and find their lives transformed by warmth, wisteria, and unexpected kindness.

title: Gosford Park
overview: A weekend shooting party at a 1930s English country house unravels when a guest is murdered — a sharp-eyed portrait of class and secrets below stairs.

title: Brief Encounter
overview: A quiet, married woman meets a doctor at a railway station; their fleeting connection over shared tea becomes the most tender and bittersweet chapter of her life.

title: The Remains of the Day
overview: An immaculately proper English butler reflects on a lifetime of service and the love he quietly refused — and the cost of perfect dignity.

title: Planet Earth: The Movie
overview: David Attenborough narrates an awe-inspiring journey through every habitat on Earth — from Arctic tundra to tropical ocean — in the landmark nature documentary.

title: The Blue Room
overview: Retired librarian Margaret Holt retreats to the Cotswolds to sort her late husband's papers and unexpectedly finds herself drawn into a local inheritance mystery.

title: Atonement
overview: A misunderstood act by a young girl one summer afternoon sets in motion a tragedy that spans decades, wartime, and an unforgivable wound to those she loved.

title: Testament of Youth
overview: The World War One memoir of Vera Brittain, who turned private grief into a lifelong crusade for peace — a luminous portrait of a woman's courage and loss.

title: Poirot: Death on the Nile
overview: Hercule Poirot investigates the murder of a beautiful heiress aboard a Nile steamer, navigating a web of jealousy, revenge, and misdirection among the passengers.

title: Nanny McPhee
overview: A mysterious, plainly dressed nanny arrives at a chaotic household with seven unruly children and a firm set of lessons — a gentle British family tale full of heart.

title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
overview: A charmingly flustered Englishman keeps crossing paths with the same American woman at a series of weddings — a warm, witty romantic comedy beloved by Jenny and Harriet.

title: Notes on a Scandal
overview: A prim school teacher befriends a new colleague unaware of her dangerous secrets — a chilling study in obsession and the quiet terror of being truly known.

title: The Lady in the Van
overview: Alan Bennett recounts the true story of the eccentric woman who lived in a van on his driveway for fifteen years — funny, sad, and wholly unforgettable.

title: The Mirror Crack'd
overview: Miss Marple investigates when a Hollywood film star's party is disrupted by a poisoning that seems aimed at someone else — adapted from the Christie novel Jenny knows by heart.

title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy
overview: A retired British intelligence officer is brought back from the cold to root out a Soviet mole at the highest levels of MI6 — a quietly devastating spy film.

title: The Winslow Boy
overview: A father wages a prolonged legal battle to clear his young son's name in Edwardian England — a stately, beautifully observed drama of honour and principle.

title: The English Patient
overview: A badly burned stranger in wartime Italy is nursed by a Canadian nurse while his memory slowly returns — a sweeping, melancholic love story.

title: Paddington 2
overview: The beloved Peruvian bear in London embarks on a search for the perfect birthday gift for his Aunt Lucy and ends up wrongly imprisoned — gentle, warm, and entirely delightful.
